task_completion.description: Open the world_survey project in the GIS application (QField).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QField application is open and displaying a map, indicating the project has been loaded.

task_completion.description: Navigate the map to the region containing Paris, Brussels, and Amsterdam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map view is zoomed in on Western Europe, specifically showing the area covering France, Belgium, and the Netherlands.
The measurement tool interface or panel (often indicated by a ruler icon or a specific measurement overlay) is active on the screen.

task_completion.description: Draw a measurement polygon (triangle) connecting Paris, Brussels, and Amsterdam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement polygon with three vertices is drawn on the map. The vertices should be positioned at or very near the locations of Paris, Brussels, and Amsterdam.

task_completion.description: Ensure the area measurement result is visible on the screen and falls within the expected range.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement result for the area is clearly visible on the screen. The value should be between 5,000 and 15,000 square kilometers (km²), or the equivalent in other units (e.g., 5,000,000,000 to 15,000,000,000 square meters).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the graphical user interface of the GIS application to perform the measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual QField app interface being used, rather than a terminal emulator, script editor, or text file.

integrity.description: The measurement result is generated by the application's native tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The area value appears within the standard measurement panel, tooltip, or overlay of the GIS application, matching the drawn polygon, rather than being typed into a generic text box or overlaid by external means.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a web browser to search for the answer.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no screenshots showing a web browser, search engine, or external website being used to look up the area between the cities.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires measuring the area of a triangle between Paris, Brussels, and Amsterdam in the 'world_survey.gpkg' project. The true geographic area is approximately 8,234 km². Acceptable measurement values shown on screen should be between 5,000 and 15,000 km² (or 5,000,000,000 to 15,000,000,000 square meters). Note that Amsterdam might not have a specific point marker from the dataset but can be located visually on the base map.
